reviews.title: Bad location, facilities and service
reviews.text:
No direct bus to the hotel, hotel had free shuttle but only 3 times a day, and it was always fully booked. Hotel should have increased the frequency of shuttle or have a larger bus, given that it was really inconvenient to get to any other areas in Hakone.  Our room was with open air bath and was a few blocks away from the reception. There were a few staff standing around but no one offered to help to transport our luggage to our room, we had to wheel our luggage across long corridors, up and down 2 elevators. The worst was that we discovered to our horror that the open air bath was only hot tap water, NOT HOT SPRING WATER!! That was never mention anywhere in the Agoda website!!! 
The dinner was good, restaurant staff were attentive. However, spread did not change much, so we had 3 nights of the same food, would be better if hotel could vary the spread more.
reviews.response:
Thank you for your candid review of your recent stay. I am sadded to read that our service and facilities doesn't sastify of your expectation. Except the shuttle bus, there are also have local bus from Odawara station and highway bus from Shinjuku which is direct to our hotel. Furthermore, We are really sorry about that our staff haven't help you wheel your luggage to your room and haven't mention the open-air bath in the room is hot water on website. Your valuable advice will share to all of our staff and We hope to have pleasure to welcome you back to Hakone on your another vacation.
Yours Sincerely,
Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori reception

reviews.title: Beware!
reviews.positives: Horrible experience.
reviews.negatives:
I was very disappointed with my stay due to both the room and the check-in process.

I specifically booked a room with a balcony and a lake view based on the photos advertised. However, I was assigned Room 114 on the ground floor, far from the main areas, and the view was completely obstructed by trees. It did not resemble the advertised view at all. Even the receptionist acknowledged that the room did not have the same lake view I had expected.

When I asked to change rooms, I was told the hotel was fully booked. I then requested a partial discount since the room clearly did not match what was advertised, but this was denied on the basis that it was still considered a “standard room.” Guests should be aware that some rooms—especially lower-level ones—may be priced the same but have significantly worse views and locations.

The location of the room was also extremely inconvenient. It required taking multiple elevators and felt very far removed from everything else on the property.

The check-in process was another major frustration. We arrived around 1:30 PM and were told our room was being cleaned and that check-in would be at 3 PM. That’s understandable—but what was unusual is that no one was allowed into their room early, even if it became ready. Instead, guests were made to wait in a crowded lobby until exactly 3 PM, when staff began handing out keys all at once. (Pictures of check-in attached.  Everyone arrived at different times yet we were all told to sit and wait until what felt like a group check in started)

The lobby became extremely packed and chaotic, feeling more like a group tour check-in than a typical hotel experience. It was surprising and poorly managed, especially since most hotels allow guests to check in individually as rooms become available.

Overall, between the misleading room expectations, lack of flexibility or compensation, and the disorganized check-in process, this was a frustrating start to our vacation.

reviews.title: Lack of guest service and unhygienic rooms
reviews.positives:
The breakfast and dinner had a lot of options and were good, do keep in mind that you have to make a reservation for each meal on the same day.
It was very helpful that the hotel organizes a shuttle bus to take you to and from the train station.
reviews.negatives:
Shortly after checking into room 420, we noticed a strong drain-like smell resembling a urine odor coming from the corner of the room. On the other side of the wall there’s the shower. The area was also visibly dusty. When we reported it to the front desk, staff were not very cooperative and did not apologize. We were initially told our only options were to either downgrade our room or pay extra for an upgrade, despite the issue being the hotel’s fault for selling a defective room.
After further discussion we were moved to another room, but unfortunately the second room also had major cleanliness problems. There were dozens of flies on the walls, ceiling, sink, and on the beds, and we even found a used bus ticket left on the floor from previous guests, suggesting the room had not been cleaned properly.
What made the experience especially disappointing was the lack of empathy and professionalism from staff throughout the situation. We understand that problems can occasionally happen in hotels, but the way this was handled was extremely poor.
This ended up being one of the worst hotel experiences we have had while traveling.

reviews.title: Good from far, far from good
reviews.text:
This hotel appears like it is going to be a magical relaxing oasis on first arrival, however it feels like a façade. They have shuttles running all day from the train station but no food or services at the hotel until after 3:00pm when you can check in. Early check in is not an option. We paid extra to have an open air bath in our room and while the open air bath was nice, the room backs onto an extremely busy and loud road with busses and trucks running day and night on it. Sitting on the balcony was not relaxing in the slightest, and you’re looking at the hotel parking lot. None of this is mentioned when booking. There was black mould and hair throughout the bathroom, and you only get 1 towel (you cannot request more). To top things off, our sheets had holes everywhere and there was a used bloody bandaid in our bed upon arrival. 

The food? Don’t get me started. IKEA food is gourmet compared to this. It truly was so foul and not fresh. The fish tasted so strongly and bad. There were about 1-2 dishes total for vegetarians (in 2025??). The breakfast was the same with no real drip coffee even. The rice tasted fishy too which was a first for Japan. There are some small eateries outside the hotel but with varying hours you can’t rely on. We didn’t eat from arriving on the hotel shutting at 12 until our dinner slot at 7:30. 

On top of this, we paid almost *** Canadian, only to see on the day of, our room had dropped to ***. We asked for some kind of price matching are we were denied. Seems unethical. 

Overall I’m truly unsure how this hotel has so many good reviews. I felt this was such a disappointing and expensive mistake, and I will not be referring anyone here.
reviews.response:
Thank you very much for staying with us at Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori.

We sincerely apologize for not meeting your expectations during your stay. Please note that additional bath towels can be delivered to your room upon request. While waiting for your room to be prepared, guests may enjoy beer, gelato, and snacks available for purchase in our lounge and shop. There are also several nearby restaurants where you can enjoy lunch.

We are truly sorry for the discomfort caused by the bathroom, bed, and other inconveniences you experienced. We take your feedback seriously and will use it to improve the areas you mentioned. We deeply regret the issues that affected your stay, and we genuinely hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back for a much more comfortable experience on your next visit to Hakone.

Rest assured that your feedback will be shared with our entire staff and management team, and we will continue working diligently to ensure a pleasant stay for all our guests.

Thank you

reviews.title: Hotel switched two rooms of our party and refused to help the group that paid for a larger room with a view, then refuse
reviews.positives: Beautiful hotel and views
reviews.negatives:
TLDR: A nice hotel that falls short in a few ways compared to what is presented/promised at time of booking, including putting people into a lesser room than what they prepaid for and then refusing to assist with some sort of refund after the fact. I would recommend considering other onsen hotels in the area.

Full Review: This is a beautiful hotel but it has some problems. The very crowded buffet is the opposite of relaxing, which an onsen hotel in a resort town should be. The buffet food is very average, nothing special. The process of getting food is chaotic and the restaurant is very, very crowded and loud. Probably because it is full of foreigners. I am one myself, but I know how to dine politely in a polite country like Japan. That said, the onsen itself is very good and relaxing. But I’m not sure that offsets the dining experience.

Second, check in was chaotic. When several people got off the shuttle bus from the train station, we were first in line. We were directed to the check in kiosks, which could not find our reservations. We then had to get in the back of the line to check in at the lobby desk. By the way, I did not see the check in kiosk work for anyone while I was there.

That would not have been so bad, but then the three separate reservations made on booking.com by our group somehow got linked. We did not all reserve the same room type and the hotel switched two of the rooms and put people in a room type they did not pay for. This is inexcusable and I don’t understand why the hotel could not figure out who should be in what room. Probably because check in was very chaotic overall.

Because we had a long trip with many hotels, it was not apparent to us at the time that the switch was made. But after reviewing their booking.com reservations, the group that was put in the lower priced, smaller room overlooking the parking lot realized they did not get what they paid for (a higher priced, larger room with a view of the lake).

reviews.title: Poor lakeview room
reviews.text:
This is to bring to the kind attention that we are extremely disappointed with the room you provided. To celebrate our wedding anniversary, we visited Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori from 5 Dec to 6 Dec 2022. During our stay, we encountered a number of disappointing service level that I think you should know and hopefully do something about it. The thing is we had confirmed the reservation of the most luxurious room of Lakeside Japanese and Western deluxe room with most expensive price compared with your other room offer. However, when we entered to the room, the view of lakeside is totally different to what you showed in your website. We thought willing to pay out more money would get an equal reward of service. The room is totally bad even compared with other room type in lower price. I wonder what is your differentiation policy of the offer of room type at your hotel for the client who is willing to pay more to get the superior quality of service. In fact, the room you offered shall be deemed as second-class room and it should be properly classified in your website.

I am writing to encourage you to improve your room type differentiation as I don’t want this experience to ruin my positive impression about your hotel group. I just hope that these problems will be solved prior  to my next visit.

Sincerely
reviews.response:
Thank you for taking your time to sending us your honest review.
We understand that your experience with us doesn’t match with your expectation.
Our hotel has many types of room, among those are the Lake Side Standard Room. This room is indeed the best room for those who prioritize view of The Lake Ashi.
The Deluxe Room with Open-Air Bath are located where there are some trees on the outside. Mainly functioned as façade for guests who use the open-air bath. Thus, the lake can't be seen as straightforward as from Standard Room.
Furthermore, for those who wish to make sure they got the best view, we also have a View Promised Plan (limited to our official website).
Your concern and feedback are very much appreciated. We will keep in mind to give a better explanation of each room types prior to arrival so that there’s no more unmet expectation in the future.
Sincerely,
Hotel Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori

reviews.title: An Overrated Onsen Hotel
reviews.text:
This establishment, despite its alluring façade and picturesque surroundings, ultimately fails to deliver a truly exceptional experience. Patrons may be drawn in by the promise of luxurious amenities and serene vistas; however, the reality often contradicts these expectations. 

First and foremost, both the buffet dinner and breakfast offerings are disappointingly limited, lacking the variety one would anticipate, while the quality of the cuisine leaves much to be desired, undermining the overall dining experience.

Moreover, the onsen experience itself does not fulfill its promise, characterized by mediocre facilities that fall short of the standard set by more esteemed establishments, compounded by notably small bathing areas that detract from the sense of relaxation and luxury..

While the hotel’s location offers breathtaking scenery, the overall value fails to align with the hyperbolic acclaim surrounding this venue, rendering it an overrated choice for travelers seeking a true onsen experience.

Thank you very much for staying with us at Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori.
Also, thank you for your feedback regarding our hotel.
We will continue to improve our buffet dining and other services so that our guests can enjoy a pleasant stay.
Thank you for posting your review.

reviews.title: There is a gap with expectations
reviews.text:
The environment is good, the room and environment are clean, it feels like a hot spring experience, and the bathrobe experience is provided. These are the advantages of the hotel.

However, the overall price/performance ratio is not high. I was looking forward to it after seeing the good reviews and recommendations of bloggers. Unexpectedly, the quality of the buffet  not as high as expected; the hot springs are also only open at certain times; also, on the day of check-out, I felt incomplete about the whole trip to Hakone. It snowed early this morning. I thought it was a symbol of luck, due to the heavy snow, all the transportations back to the city/Odawara were stopped, it is extremely inconvenient for passengers travelling by transportation though there are 3 designated shuttle per day. I believe it is not the first time all the transportations were being stopped . I hope that the hotel will have plan for  guests return to the city in heavy snow.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text:
I will occasionally indulge myself on a 5* hotel so sadly, when this was priced like one for the Sakura season, I thought it would meet my standards.  My expectations were unfortunately not met as  the hotel just  has too many people carted through from the buffet dinners/breakfasts and check-ins.  So if you are wanting quiet and peace, this is not the hotel even though the nature outside has it.  
The rooms are clean and adequate.  Half board is necessary since there is no other restaurants nearby and there is only one large self-service canteen at the hotel.  There is no personal attention at all, and you have to retrieve your own toiletries from a communal section. Service is with a smile, but this establishment makes me think that I visited a farm factory just because it was so crowded.  Missed the 5* personal touch and facilities but paid for one. They should really install some sound proofing in the large atrium as it gets too noisy during check-in. Literally the hotel attendant had to yell at me in order to tell her introduction.
reviews.response: Thank you for coming to Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori. We apologize for not being able to meet your wishes. There are many customers who come during this holiday season so they have to spend a lot of time waiting. We will have a chance to set up a chat service.Thank you .

reviews.title: Good food, but not recommended on rainy days
reviews.text:
We enjoyed Hanaori's breakfast and dinner very much. However, unfortunately, there was nothing to do on a rainy day: No gym, no karaoke, only TV in the room.
Also only one Onsen. The reception is on the 3rd floor, and we had to climb up the stairs with luggage. Only one elevator marked L for Reception/Lobby, but other elevators had no buttons to indicate where the Reception was. It took a while to find our way to the Reception from our room. The room was very clean and nice with balcony.

reviews.title: Disappointment staff attitude
reviews.text:
I was looking forward to a comfortable and convenient stay at ashinoko hanaoriI but unfortunately, our experience was marred by poor service and inconvenient facilities.

Firstly, the concierge service during check-in was subpar. We were rushing to meet our dinner reservations, but the staff seemed unbothered by our time constraint. When we asked for directions to the restaurant, the staff member who led us there displayed a poor attitude, failing to smile or greet us properly.

Furthermore, the hotel's layout proved to be inconvenient for our group, which included elderly members and young children. The rooms were far from the lobby and difficult to navigate.

To make matters worse, the water heater temperature was inconsistent, making it uncomfortable for us to shower.

Overall, I was disappointed by the lack of attention to detail and customer service at ashinoko hanaoriI. While the hotel's location and amenities may be satisfactory, the poor service and inconvenient facilities made our stay less than enjoyable.
reviews.response: Thank you for staying at Hakone-Ashinoko Hanaori. We feel very much sorry to hear that you felt disappointed with poor services given by our staff. We will strengthen training of our staff to improve our services, such as quick and faster check-in, assistance of guests to carry their luggage to their rooms, etc., so that this hotel will be more comfortable to stay.

reviews.title: 靚靚溫泉旅館，但酒店員工冷漠
reviews.text: 如果想去大涌谷和坐盧之湖遊覽，呢間酒店就最方便，因為隔離就係盧之湖碼頭和纜車站(桃園站）步行三分鐘即達。溫泉同相片一樣咁靚，大堂有免費咖啡、凍飲提供，附近冇便利店和食店（碼頭有間餐廳，不過得好少選擇，亦唔知收幾點），二樓有幾部飲品機，可以自己比錢買，但零食要自己帶，因為附近真係乜都冇得賣，所以大家一定要訂早餐和晚餐。溫泉除咗基本洗頭沖涼用品外，仲有洗面、卸妝水、爽膚、潤膚品樣樣齊，連烝面機都有，女仕們亦可以在二樓自己挑選浴衣，真係一分錢一分貨，正。自助餐選擇不是太多，質素算ok.  房間舒適，浴室乾濕分離。最唔鍾意就係員工好冷漠，特別餐廳的員工，一點笑容都沒有，問我明天幾點吃早餐，我哋揀8:00，他指一指張紙，黑口黑面咁話，8:00 係繁忙時間，成間酒店的員工都欠缺笑容、欠主動，只有司機最友善。
reviews.response: Thank you for staying at our hotel and also for your comment. We regret the attitude our staff took during your stay. We will try our very best efforts to improve our services. We apologize the inconvenience caused, and we look forward to seeing you again in future.

Nice hotel, but the rooms can be very far and complicated to reach from the lobby. We had to take to elevators and walk quite far to get to our room. 
Dinner may not be suitable for everyone, it was mostly fish and sea food which I don’t eat so it was hard for me to find something to eat.
Also, breakfast is ending very early at 9:30 am and you can wait on line to get in because all the guests of the hotel have to eat in two and half hours.
Overall nice hotel but needs improvements.
reviews.negatives: Dinner, long walk to the room.

Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori reception

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.positives:
The room was nice.
Comfy bed, pleasant private bath, the little details of the slippers complementary water, tea and shrimp cracks.
Shower was pleasant.
Really near by Togendai station.
The onsen is nice, interesting experience, good facility.
The decor and the views from the hotel are stunning.
reviews.negatives:
Half of the Ryokan is under construction so to get to my room and from it to the restaurant, vending machine, reception and onsen was at least a 5 minutes walk. That was very confusing and unpleasant.
Receptionist didn't speak very well English and wasn't trying their best to help, I'd even say somewhat aggressive. Even when I called reception from my room call got disconnected a few times and they didn't bother to call back.
Buffet food was okay, there was plenty but no dish stood out for me.
They don't allow tattoos in the onsen and charge you extra for cover stickers.
Remote control showed they have Netflix but they don't.
Don't expect to see Mount Fuji from hotel.
The hotel under achieved my expectations and I will not stay here again.
My private bath was facing the street and I felt there was no privacy as people walking by and can look.

reviews.title: Top architecture design let down by services that don't live up to the price charged
reviews.text:
Good
1. Design of the property is v good. The view at the lobby, the room are comfy
2. Location being next to Hakone rope way cable car & pirate ship docking bay making it convenient to get back to the hotel & ride the hotel bus to odawara station.
Bad
1. Services not up to the price charged. Over price by 30%
2. No potter service. Arrived at 1430. Hotel bus departed at 1330 & the next is at 1630 so we decided to take the public bus up. The ride is a far 60 min away. Once reached, we still have to  push our own luggage up a hill all by ourselves. Even after checked in at the counter we still have to push the luggage to our rooms by ourselves 
2. Meal experience was mediocre at best. Buffett for dinner and breakfast. 
3. It was my daughter's birthday. Informed the staff & during dinner, staff served us desserts with happy birthday message at the end of meal but with food salvaged from the buffet which we just finished! Zero effort! & we can't take just the gift salvaged food  the room.
reviews.response: Thank you for taking the time to share your review with us. We appreciate your valuable feedback as it helps us identify the areas we can improve the guest experience. We hope you will come back soon in future. Best Regards. Hanaori Frontdesk.

reviews.title: Above average

reviews.title: Excellent Hotel
reviews.text: The hotel has exceeded my expectations. It was a bit hard to get to by bus but is was worth it. I did not get out of the hotel at all and enjoyed it to the max. They offer you to wear Yokata all around the place. I enjoyed the open air onsen we had in our room. The dinner was mostly uncooked sea food which was not my favorite, but I found a few selections of veggie options. You will need to get all what you need before arriving to the hotel because its really far from the Yomoto station where you first arrive at Hakone.
reviews.response: Thank you for your feedback regarding your stay. We hope that you enjoyed wearing Yukata and the open-air bath as well. We are sorry that the buffet was below your expectation. We will try to improve our service. We're looking forward to your next visit. Best regards! Hanaori Reception

reviews.text: Its a great locaton with a fantastic view. The cable cart and to the boat is just 2minutes walk too. The place is super clean, and the fecilities for the hot springs are also really nice and clean and they give you everything u might need. We both had a small tattoo and you can buy tattoo patches to cover them for 200yen. Breakfast and dinner were very versatile and super tasty. The thing that let down the whole trip is that no one told us about the free complimentary shuttle back to Odawara station that is like 1-2hours on a public bus...like NO ONE. It seems all the locals got on that bus but us as foreigners were left out. When we overheard that there is a bus and asked we been told is completely full.... its a big let down especially with big lagguages when you travel around Japan.

The price of alcohol was charged with high price, my husband ordered a medium glass of draft beer with 900yen. The food was same every day, even the meat and seafood which supposed to be more specifical were also same (not very fresh fish and not good quality pork). There were only water in the fridge in our room. I especially like the old guy at the door, who takes care of the umbrella. He was like a typical old Japanese man.

reviews.title: A comprehensive place to go!
reviews.text:
The building is beautiful, the interior is clean, and many of the staff are foreigners, but they can speak Japanese. 
But the best part is the buffet meal. There's all-you-can-drink alcohol, and the buffet includes items like salmon roe and eel. 
The view from the hotel is nice, and the price is not high either. 
Everything seemed perfect, but there was one issue... 
The garment meant to be worn over the samue in the room had not been washed... 
I was surprised by the curry stains and the horrible smell. 
That is unacceptable. I realized this after having them bring me a different size via the intercom since they didn't have my size, so I didn't mention it before leaving. 
If it weren't for that, it would have been perfect...

reviews.title: Secluded retreat
reviews.text:
We stayed here with two young kids (2&4) and my mother. Unfortunately we booked a bit late for cherry blossom season so there weren't many good options left - this hotel was recommended in a Reddit thread.

It was my first time in Hakone so I didn't realize how far the hotel is away from the nearest shinkansen station (Odawara). It's a 40 minute drive, and the hotel does offer limited shuttle service but I didn't realize it before nor did they tell me about it. It's about $100 to cab to the hotel via taxi. We did end up taking the free shuttle back to Odawara (there is a 10am and 1pm shuttle), but just be aware.

The location is great if you want a peaceful retreat away from people. It's in a hamlet next to Lake Ashinoko and if our kids hadn't been sick while we were there, we would have done more outdoor exploration. But if you want more touristy things to do, don't stay here because it's at least a 10-15 cab ride anywhere and it's very hard to find a cab - you'd have to book it with the hotel first.

That being said, it's a great place to stay if you just want to relax and hang out in the onsen. We had a good time, and the onsen was never crowded (maybe non Asian people don't like being totally naked in front of others, but there were very few non Asian people in the onsen), and you can also book their private onsen. 

One issue with their service relative to our general experience in Japan is that they have a lot of non-Japanese staff that may be seasonal. The Japanese staff who are permanent are well trained and very polite/thoughtful, but we had a range of experiences with the seasonal/non-Japanese staff. Nothing unpleasant but when you have the gold standard of Japanese service as a contrast, it's quite obvious.

Nevertheless, I would recommend this place if your goal is to stay a few days and relax. Breakfast and dinner are included and the breakfast is very good, better than the dinner. There are lots of families with young kids so it's definitely kid friendly as well.
reviews.response:
Thank you for staying with Hakone, Ashinoko Hanaori, also we appriciate you for sharing your experience.
Our hotel is far away from the station. thus we provide shuttle service from Odawara Station. 
You can make the booking for our shuttle bus prior to your reservation, so please tell us anytime for your future visit.
As for the other transportation option, you can take the public transportation and if you bought Hakone Free Pass.
You could use the bus, ship, gondola, and all of the transportation in Hakone to go to tourist spots in Hakone. 
Again, thank you for your constructive feedbacks, if you come again in the future please feel free to contact us.
We hope to improve your experience with us in the future.
We will be waiting for your next visit.
Hakone,  Ashinoko Hanaori. Accommodation Section.

reviews.text: Hakone town is amazing!!! Need to spend more time there walking around and exploring the good food and lakes around. Time spend going up to hotel by train, cable car was a disaster. Took almost 2 hours to reach the hotel. Last cable car is at 5pm, be careful of your time keeping. Hotel room was amazing, unfortunately it was 2 blocks away from front desk. Our room was with open air bath, luckily, coz both me n my wife has tattoos which was not allowed in public hot spring bath pools. Breakfast and dinner buffet was included, so-so food but dinner amazed me off with Kyoho grapes on the dessert buffet line. Going to Gotemba outlet was easy. New hotel just celebrating 1st anniversary. More anniversaries to come.

reviews.title: Nice and modern onsen hotel with great view
reviews.text: We stayed in a Japanese/Western room with lake view. Everything was great but strange thing they build the toilet door at the bed area instead of the toilet so it was inconvenient to change yiur clothes if you are staying with someone. Only local tv channels available. Onsen facilities was great. Buffet breakfast and dinner was good but they serve exactly the same dishes for both days. Even the meat and seafood were similar which was pork and fish. I would expect them to rotate e.g seafood one night fish one night prawn/ squid etc. For guests staying more than one night, u get bored with the food by the 2nd day.

reviews.title: Lovely - could have stayed longer.
reviews.text:
The hotel itself is lovely. Included dinner/breakfast. Dinner was amazing. We read a lot of crazy reviews about the food, but there was so much variety I can't see how you could complain (& I am a pescatarian). We had a normal room but then booked a private onsen after dinner for 40 minutes at 2000yen (20usd) which was well worth it as it meant as a couple we could bathe together.
Calling the hotel beforehand and trying to communicate in English is difficult. The hotel provides a free shuttle bus from Odawara station which saves a lot of trouble - if you can manage to book it. The first time we called we were told it was full. I read other reviews that said this also. However, we rang back within 5 minutes, got a different staff member and got a reservation on the shuttle... not sure why the staff are trained to say this when our shuttle bus had at least 9 more empty seats. 
Another piece of advice - ring and book a dinner time before you arrive. Overall, a lovely hotel :)
reviews.response:

We're glad to know you had a good time with us.
The reservation of the shuttle changed a lot and we always have same day cancellation, it could be the reason you had a question about it. Anyway we'll try our best on giving equal information.

Thank you for your feedback.

Best regards, Hanaori reception

reviews.title: Staff service exceptional, everything else standard
reviews.text:
Overall the stay was pleasant. We booked for one night stay with private onsen and dinner and breakfast inclusive. Unfortunately on our checkin day the weather was foggy and rainy, so we couldn’t see any views. The lobby was packed with customers and the hotel started allowing us into rooms at 2.30pm (ahead of the official check in time of 3pm) - this was appreciated. 

The public onsen facility was standard in Japan and I wouldn’t say anything was exceptional. There were a few customers not observing the onsen protocol of showering before bathing, which made the experience slightly unpleasant. At check in, the staff did ask whether we were familiar with onsen protocol (given we are not Japanese) so I wouldn’t discount the effort, but maybe more reminders at onsen facilities in multiple languages? 

Dinner and breakfast was in my honest opinion a bit disappointing, after having been to quite many onsen hotels in Japan. Both meals offer a combination of western and eastern dishes, which unfortunately in my view were not quite authentic. The diversity in food offerings probably added more bad than good in my view to the experience - I’d rather have my typical kaiseki dinner with nabe and etc and breakfast with TKG (they don’t serve raw eggs for breakfast! I’m not sure how to enjoy rice without raw eggs for breakfast!). 

The staff was very friendly and helpful and I think this is the best part of the experience. 

Our room with private onsen is on level 1 - I’m not sure if I enjoy being on level 1 as there is literally no view and on a rainy day, being on a lower floor only makes it darker and more gloomy. 

Unlike some of the other reviewers, I think I’d hesitate to return - if you want authentic Japanese onsen ryokan experience I’d recommend somewhere with kaiseiki dinner and sleep on futon on tatami.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text:
This trip was to express my gratitude to my family, who supported me through my serious illness for eight years. Coincidentally, the permission for the trip was granted in the month of our wedding anniversary, so I decided to stay at Hanawari, recommended by an acquaintance. Since I had to avoid crowds as much as possible, I consulted with the staff before my stay. They provided suggestions to avoid congestion from check-in to breakfast, dinner, and check-out, which gave me peace of mind before arrival. 

The room was a Japanese-Western style DX lakeside room with a balcony and an outdoor bath, and it was clean and very comfortable. Unfortunately, it was raining, so we missed the scenery of Lake Ashi. It is a bit of a walk to the front desk, but it didn't bother me at all. The reception staff were friendly, and the buffet staff were quick and efficient. The other staff members greeted us warmly, which was pleasant. 

Dinner was served in small bowls, making it easy to eat, and the flavors were delicious. However, it would have been better if there were sushi, tempura, hamburgers, or curry, which the kids would enjoy. The dessert was very satisfying. Breakfast included delicious fresh vegetable juice, and the bread and meat and potato stew were tasty. I particularly liked the squid salt-preserve and fermented black soybeans. The pickled plum was quite sour, so I thought it would go well with rice porridge. A fried egg would have been the icing on the cake.

I wasn’t able to use the hot spring due to a doctor's restriction, but my wife mentioned that the water was a bit lukewarm, which might not satisfy those who prefer warmer water. Our high school daughter thought the temperature was just right. My wife also stated she would have liked a bit more variety in the amenities. 

It’s just a 2-3 minute walk to Tōgendai, so the pirate ship on Lake Ashi and the ropeway at Owakudani weren’t much of a hassle even with the intermittent rain, and I appreciated the availability of umbrellas and towels. 

After this stay, I think it would be nice if the hotel had a game center, karaoke, or a bar for fun at night, but I feel it suits families, couples, or those like us who want a quiet and relaxing time. 

One disappointment this time was that we couldn’t enjoy the water terrace and foot bath due to the rain, and we couldn’t take a walk around Kuzuryu Shrine and Lake Ashi. Since we couldn’t do the things we wanted, I hope to be able to return to Hanawari for a second chance next time.

The dining options near the hotel were limited, and due the large volume of guests, the hotel restaurant was busy and often there was a wait. It was also difficult to schedule taxis without significant planning. Still, a lovely spot - you just need to think about some of the logistics well in advance because some choices are limited due to the remote nature of where you are.

In addition, the supply of cabs is not keeping up with the demand for cabs in Hakone, making it very difficult to dispatch cabs on the day of the tour.If you are considering taking a cab, we recommend that you make a reservation in advance.

The Pros: dinner was great, many varieties,  room spacious, corridors and lift lobby very clean and nicely decorated, staff helped departing guests to take photo of the lake and the hotel lobby was nice touch.

reviews.positives: the food was excellent, bathing facilities wonderful, and the beds quite comfortable.
reviews.negatives: I thought there would be different food options each day, but they were mostly the same. All very good, but the same two days in a row.

reviews.title: One of the best views in hakone
reviews.text: Definitely the highlight of our recent trip. The views of lake ashi is very spectacular! Food during the dinner buffet is also excellent. Room is clean and spacious. I just wish there were other things to do for kids like a pool or arcade area like other onsen hotels.

reviews.title: For the price - luggage should be transfer to your room & maybe have another onsen for tattoo??
reviews.positives: Gorg property and great view.
reviews.negatives: Couldn’t use public onsen cause of tattoos so had to do the private which was fine but I had to pay extra for that as well and you have to pay for late checkout - shuttle didn’t pickup till 3:30- but check out is at 10. Rainy weather so we had to just hang out . Also did luggage transfer- they don’t bring your luggage to you’re room so that was strange for such a nice hotel

reviews.title: Family travel
reviews.text: The best resort hotel to enjoy the breathtaking views of Lake Ashi is located within a 5-minute walk from the bus stop. The transportation is very convenient! The check-in process was smooth and without issues. The proud foot bath facility overlooks the entire Lake Ashi, allowing guests to relax easily. The rooms are spacious and very clean! The guest rooms, restaurant, and public areas offer beautiful lake views, and both dinner and breakfast are served buffet-style with a wide variety that is very satisfying. Additionally, the dinner drinks are included in the dinner cost, so no extra charges apply, making it easy to enjoy. As for the bathroom, the open-air bath allows guests to soak while enjoying the view of Lake Ashi, and there are two types of saunas available for me to experience during my stay. The scenery is excellent, the food is delicious, and the bathroom is great. I sincerely hope to stay here again.
reviews.response: Thank you for staying at Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori. We truly appreciate you for taking time to write such a detailed a valuable review. We are very delighted that you had a great stay with us and enjoyed everything we provided. Please note that our buffet menu and hotel view is changes with seasons. The next time you visit, we are sure that there will be fresh experiences waiting. We look forward to meeting you again! thank you very much for your kind comments and review.

reviews.title: Beautiful hotel, could use some improvements to bed comfort
reviews.positives: The location is perfect to see all the sights in Hakone, the breakfast and dinner buffets were high quality and diverse, the views from the hotel were beautiful.
reviews.negatives: The bathroom really smelled like damp, which impacted our stay. The beds are very uncomfortable with only one (hard) pillow per bed.

reviews.title: Must stay. Perfect ending to a Japan trip
reviews.text:
I stayed here for two nights at the end of a two week Japan trip and this truly made my trip.  So relaxing.  Perfectly located. Recommend doing some of the other tourist spots around. Not much to do in town but you don’t need any more.  Loved the outdoor onsen (pay attention to the signs for which sex goes where. Does stay the same) 

Breakfast and dinner buffets exceptional with all you can drink too.  

Kimonos/kumatas provided in every room and so easy to walk around the hotel wearing them.  Very comfortable and everyone gets onboard.
reviews.response: Thank you for staying at Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori. We truly appreciate you for giving us a full rating of review. We are very delighted that you had a great stay with us and enjoyed everything we provided. Please note that our buffet menu and hotel view is changes with seasons. The next time you visit, we are sure that there will be fresh experiences waiting. We look forward to meeting you again! thank you very much for your kind comments and review.

reviews.title: A large luxury style resort, isolated from the action and with so-so food.
reviews.positives: This is a luxury style resort with lovely rooms and a nice onsen. Having a buffet breakfast and dinner meant not having to choose a restaurant (nice after a couple weeks in Japan).
reviews.negatives: Because the resort has so many guests, it has a busy buffet with mediocre food (some good, some not). The hotel hallways are always playing this same piped in music and there are people everywhere (big resort). It also is very isolated from activities & is a long bus ride from the station (I could not rent a decent bike), so I ended up feeling like I'd made a mistake on this booking. Book this place if you love this style of resort. It wasn't for me (to be fair my husband liked it).

reviews.title: Parent-child trip
reviews.text: I stayed with my daughter. The room was clean, and the private onsen service was nice. The view of the sunset was beautiful, and the dinner buffet was very luxurious. I found myself wishing I had a bigger appetite.

In addition, on our day of departure when it was raining heavily, one of our staff gestured to quickly drive out   so as to make space for incoming vehicle at the arrival point.

reviews.title: One of the best stay in Japan
reviews.text: Great room and meals. Although the variety is almost the same for 2 days. quality is there and the staff is great.
reviews.response: Thank you very much for staying with us at Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori. We are delighted to hear that you enjoyed your stay with us. reviews.title: Very accomodating, peaceful location, great meals!
reviews.positives: We had a great time at Hanaori with the kids. It’s a bit of a trip from Odawara station, we weren’t able to secure a free shuttle to the hotel at a suitable time for us (book early) and the local bus was a bit of a wait so we just caught a taxi. They provide kimonos for all, and advise that you can wear them around the hotel. We weren’t able surprised at how many guests were wearing them at meals. Speaking of meals, as the other reviews suggested, was great. Good mix of local and western to keep the kids happy. Nice and close to the lake where we hired paddle boats and also the cable car station to head up. The open air museum is a must! The kids especially loved it. Loved Hakano and Hanaori!
reviews.negatives: Nothing. Maybe more snacks in the bar would be appreciated.

reviews.title: Perfect Onsen
reviews.text:
Incredible service from front desk team helping my mom to get her lost and found item from the hotel to the city by cash on delivery. Thank you for the prompt reply via email, the staff Moe Okuzum is so kind and make me feeling safe!

The concierge boy is helpful too that helping us to use the machine to check out.

Beautiful surroundings, good food and spacious Onsen Pool!

And it is so closed to the Cable Car and Sightseeing Cruise!!! Just a 4 mins walk. 

Highly recommended.

reviews.title: Memorable experience
reviews.text: I had a very good experience. Two small negative things to remark, however: 1) very flaky internet access, 2) occasional strong hydrogen sulfur (rotten egg) smell in the hotel room. 1) can be fixed by hotel; 2) only mother nature to blame, as the hotel is close to active volcanic area which emits H2S naturally. Therefore, for people with strong sensitivity to sulfur, not recommendable.

reviews.text: I landed in Haneda, rented a car, and visited a ryokan in Hakone. I reserved a room with an outdoor bath, and the room facilities were clean and nice. There was also an outlet nearby where I could see Mount Fuji well, so it was nice to shop while enjoying the view of Mount Fuji.

reviews.title: Relaxing stay for 1 night in Hakone
reviews.positives:
We greatly enjoyed our stay at Ashinoko Hanaori. The lobby and the view it offered were lovely. Our open-air private onsen was absolutely delightful and the view from the room was spectacular.

The beds were a little too soft for me and the overall vibe was rather touristy. The buffet was a little mid, but it did its job well enough. My husband really enjoyed the available sashimi.

reviews.title: More than I thought.
reviews.text: I visited to celebrate my daughter's birthday. I had ordered a cake, but it was a bit different from what I expected, so that was disappointing. The dinner buffet was very delicious, and having alcohol as a free drink was great. The facility was generally clean, but the restrooms had a slight odor. It would have been nice to have a more pleasant scent. The bath was spacious and very open, and I was able to relax in it. It's convenient to be a 5-minute walk to the pirate ship boarding area. The staff was very friendly, but I was disappointed that a few of the cleaning staff were not smiling or greeting. I have found a place that I would like to visit again to relax.

reviews.title: Recommended stay in the Lake Ashi area
reviews.positives: Great place to relax for a couple of days between cities. Lovely onsen facilities, men and women switch sides each day to experience each side (slighty different). We traveled as a couple, but it was definitely a family friendly place. Not an issue for us, but that could be a deterrent for others that want a more private experience. We had a room with a private onsen bath which was great to have in addition to the onsens shared by guests. Everything was well stocked, staff was friendly, and the easy access to the ropeway was fantastic. Shuttle coordinated by the hotel was great to get from/to Odawara Station.
reviews.negatives: Not too much else to do in the area, but really the point of staying in this area was to be more peaceful anyway. While the buffet style dinner was fantastic in terms of amount of options and great tasting flavors, if you were staying here for 2+ days, the options could get quite repetitive.

reviews.title: I recommend it.
reviews.text: I used it for the first time on a family memorial trip. On the day when heavy snow caused the Tomei Expressway to be closed, we somehow managed to arrive, but when I called the front desk that morning, they told me that free cancellations and date changes were possible, which made me feel relieved. It might have been due to the heavy snow, but there weren't too many guests at the hotel, so we were able to enjoy both the large communal bath and the meals. The dinner and breakfast buffets were wonderful, with everything tasting great. There was also all-you-can-drink alcoholic beverages, which made my family very happy. For someone like me who struggles with hot baths at day-use hot spring facilities, the temperature setting was perfect and allowed me to relax. Thanks to the heavy snow, we got to enjoy a snowy bath. The only unfortunate thing was that the sofa that looked like it was in the water basin outside the lobby was not usable. As shown in the pictures, although it couldn't be helped on the day of the heavy snow, the weather was nice and the temperature went up the next day, so I thought they could have cleared the snow from it. Therefore, I have to give the service a minus one.
reviews.response: この度は、箱根・芦ノ湖 はなをりにご宿泊いただきまして誠にありがとうございます。貴重なお時間をいただき、詳細にレビューを記載していただきましたこと重ねて御礼申し上げます。　ご滞在中、ごゆっくりお過ごしいただけた様子が伺え私共も大変嬉しく存じます。箱根の名湯と芦ノ湖の雄大な眺望とのコラボレーションが望める温泉や、旬の食材を使用した料理を堪能していただけたら幸いでございます。当館のビュッフェのメニューは季節に応じて変わってまいりますので、次回のご来館もきっとお楽しみいただけるかと思います。またのご来館をスタッフ一同心よりお待ち申し上げております。ご投稿ありがとうございました。

reviews.title: Fantastic for travellers
reviews.text:
We opted for the open air bath and it was fantastic. While the room is relatively small, I understand this is more for staycay kind so people usually pack light. The location of the accoms was great too. A stone throw away from Togendai ropeway and pirate ship. The dedicated bus that brings us to and fro Odawara station to the accoms was great too. We didnt drive so, I cant imagine how we gonna carry our bulky luggages to get there. Once you enter, you are greeted by the staffs and with great view and foot baths. 
Dinner and breakfast were a bit repetitive for our 3 days long stay. Otherwise still great.

reviews.title: Mother's birthday trip
reviews.text: I made a reservation because it had high ratings and looked good when I searched around Hakone. The room and common areas were very clean, and I was able to spend a very comfortable time there. The meals were plentiful in variety, and my mother was also pleased. However, one downside is that the layout of the building is complex, and I think those who are directionally challenged may get lost. My room was far from the elevator, so moving around was a hassle. I would appreciate it if you could improve things by providing more guidance within the building. The shuttle bus from Odawara Station was also helpful. I would like to stay again if I have the opportunity.

reviews.positives: public and private onsen options, the view over the lake
reviews.negatives: food options and the dining hall vibe

reviews.title: A wonderful stay
reviews.text:
My daughter (15) and I really enjoyed our stay. We were in a twin room with outside bath  which provided just enough room for the two of us. We travelled with just a backpack as we only stayed 2 nights but it would have been a tight squeeze if we had brought our medium sized suitcases. The bath on the balcony was lovely and hot and full at all times (the water replenishes when you step out of the bath). The onsen was amazing. Three indoor pools and an outside one plus a steam room. It was a real treat each night to go and use the onsen before bed. There are also massage chairs before you enter the onsen but these were not a gentle massage, more of a firm, deep massage.  The location was perfect. Beautiful views across the lake and minuets from buses and the ropeway plus private ships. If you start the ‘loop’ using the ropeway before  10am you’ll be rewarded with very few people on the transport (everyone is going in the opposite direction). 

Whilst the food was plentiful and we really enjoyed trying everything, I do think there could have been more Japanese food .

reviews.negatives: We got the lake view room but our entire view of the lake was blocked by trees. They did well to accomplish us by giving us the top floor but still the view was anything but desirable

reviews.title: Beautiful Place
reviews.text:
The hotel is nice and beautiful view from the lobby. The onsen is really good. The location is near from Togendai station where several kind of transportation are provided. The staff who is in charge for replying message to customer need to be trained because the respond is so slow. I booked the shuttle bus from Odawara station to the hotel. We were there 2 hours earlier from the schedule 2-15 pm and waited. I asked the hotel how could I find the car and the driver but there was no reply until 2.15  We did not see bus or car with the printed Ashinoko Hanaori. We started to be panic and finally found the car with the printed Orix hotel. There is no information at all in advance that it would be Onix hotel. How do we know that?
By the time we arrived and checked in, the receptionist informed that our room is lake view but surprised to find out that it is garden view and not lake view. When I asked to change the room into lake view as he promised, he told me that the available on is parking view !! So be aware in advance about which view you want to have when booking.
reviews.response: Thank you for staying at Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori. We deeply apologize for inconvenience happening to you, we already checked it but there is a gap time when you send the messages from Agoda. Regarding hotel room, there`s a room that have a public bath and without public bath. facing the lake side and facing the mountain. So please be careful when you choose the room type. We relieved that you liked the view from the hotel, and enjoyed our hot spring. Thank you for your detailed reviews. We are always waiting for your return here.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.negatives: The pictures made it look like the view would be of the lake, but our view of the lake was blocked by trees.

reviews.title: Amazing staff and stunning views!
reviews.text:
This place is akin to a modern Western-styled ryokan, where you find beds and tatamis in your room and get to don on yukatas after your time at the onsen. Speaking of onsen, just be mindful that they’re pretty strict on the no tattoos policy. The tattoo stickers sold at the reception weren’t the best too as they were not easy to apply on your skin. 

The buffet dinner and breakfast provided were really good even though the menu was the same for all 3 nights that we spent here. I guess the main highlight was the free flow alcohol where you get to have your fill of beer, Japanese whisky, spirits and wine!

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: Breakfast and dinner buffet both are good
reviews.negatives: Bath towel not the full size one, TV and out door hot tub needs to improve for bugs.

reviews.title: Great stay!
reviews.text:
A bit pricey but so worth it as a spot to unwind after being in Japan's cramped hotel rooms & crowded spots 😞 The hotel staff were also so friendly, accommodating and efficient. Only regret is not staying one more night! 

Hotel is a bit out of the way but they provide transport to and from Odawara Station. Just remember to book it in advance to get the slot you want- I just messaged the hotel on Agoda and they got back to me on the same day! 

Even though we had a hot stone bath in the room, we still went ahead to enjoy the onsen facilities. My partner and I both have tattoos but we just purchased the temporary skin tattoos over the counter with the friendly staff- it is about 200yen palm sized for 1 sticker, so I would recommend purchasing a few in the convenience store before heading there as the size is a bit small

reviews.title: From me, who is visiting the most popular accommodations.
reviews.text:
This place was amazing. The balance of the bath, the food, and the room was just right. I was looking for a nice place. To be honest, I have only been to places with good ratings and reviews. If it's Hakone, then it's Ten⭕️en, and if it's Atami, then it's A⭕️o, and Ju⭕️kan.

Everywhere has its good points, but if you don't compromise, you'll end up thinking, "Hmm?" during your trip.

Initially, Hanaori had a buffet, and everything really suited my taste. It was a bit on the rich side, but I liked it, and everything was delicious.

The room had an outdoor bath. If you prefer a spacious room, I think it's better to choose a standard one. My room was filled up with two beds. However, there were other rooms that were spacious. The larger the room for work purposes, the better for me. After seeing the other rooms, I thought I should be more careful with room selection next time.

The bath was one you could think of coming back to after trying it once. The in-room bath was new for me, but that was fine in its own way.

With this, I found a place with a good overall balance.

I still have many other places to visit, but I definitely want to come here at least once a year.

reviews.title: Perfect for a break during sightseeing!
reviews.text:
The reason I chose this hotel was that I was drawn in by the beautiful scenery photos, and I had thought, "I want to visit this place once," and this time I finally had the opportunity to go.

About the room
The room we chose had an open-air bath, which was great because we could bathe whenever we liked! Also, the large communal bath was spacious, and if it wasn't crowded, I think it would be very comfortable. In particular, the water infused with slices of cypress was relaxing with its fragrance. It felt clean, and the facilities were well-equipped, which satisfied me.

The room itself was also spacious, providing a comfortable atmosphere to spend time in.

About the meals
This plan included both dinner and breakfast, both of which were buffet-style. There was a rich variety of meat, fish, and other menu items, all of which were delicious.

Overall evaluation
It was a very nice hotel, and while the accommodation fee was a bit on the higher side, I felt it was worth the satisfaction received for that amount. It was relatively close to the station, and the location and view were both very good. It was a hotel I would definitely like to stay at again next time!

reviews.title: Fantastic Amenities and Food, but Room View and Transport Could Be Better
reviews.text:
We had a great experience overall, thanks to the excellent amenities and top-notch staff service. The buffet-style meals for both breakfast and dinner were delicious and offered plenty of variety. The location is perfect for visiting the lake, pirate ship, and ropeway, though it's a bit inconvenient to travel to the main station.

However, transportation was challenging—taxis were hard to find, and the shuttle bus service filled up quickly. Additionally, we were disappointed with our room arrangement, as we didn’t get a lake view and faced the parking lot instead. Luckily, the beautiful lake view could still be enjoyed from the lobby and restaurant areas. The yukata arrangement was a nice touch that added to the experience.

Overall, it’s a lovely stay, but better transport options and room views would make it perfect!
reviews.response:
Thank you very much for staying at Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori. We truly appreciate that you took the time to share such a detailed review. We are delighted to hear that you had a wonderful stay with us and that you were able to fully enjoy the Hakone transportation system.
Regarding the lake-facing room, we sincerely apologize that it did not meet your expectations. Please kindly note that all Casual Twin Rooms face the mountains and parking area, while only the Standard Rooms and Japanese Deluxe Rooms offer lake views. Nevertheless, we are glad you were still able to enjoy the beautiful scenery of Lake Ashi from our lobby and restaurant.
We look forward to welcoming you again in the future. Thank you once again for your kind post, and we hope to see you next time!

Kids loved the hot foot bath at the lobby. We had a nice hot foot bath while admiring the amazing scenery. Small towels are provided so that you can wipe your feet dry after your foot bath. Complimentary drinks are provided at check in. 

Price was inclusive of buffet breakfast and buffet lunch. Mainly Japanese cuisine was served (which I love), but the kids did not appreciate Japanese food as much as the adults do.

reviews.title: It's great that there are many free services available.

The sashimi, including bluefin tuna and sweet shrimp, was delicious, and there was a good variety of desserts that I thoroughly enjoyed! Unfortunately, the view wasn't great due to the rain, but the bathtub in the room was nice. However, I noticed that the Wi-Fi was weak, which made it very difficult to connect, possibly due to the rain. Also, the floor in the washing area of the room’s bath was slippery and felt dangerous, and the distance from the room to the bath and the restaurant was long! Those were the only three things that concerned me, but overall, it was a satisfying trip with great hot springs, meals, and staff service! I would like to go again.

reviews.text:
Togendai ​Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori offers a truly wonderful stay defined by great service and high-quality accommodations. The staff were very friendly and helpful throughout our visit, making us feel completely welcome and cared for.

​The rooms are a major plus. They are big, very clean, and provide ample space to relax comfortably.

​The location is also unbeatable, situated right next to Togendai station, which makes it extremely convenient for catching the Hakone Ropeway and the sightseeing cruise ship on Lake Ashi. Furthermore, the dining is superb. Both the dinner and breakfast were excellent buffet experiences, offering a vast and satisfying selection of Japanese and Western dishes.

​The quality and variety of the dining alone, combined with the helpful staff and lovely rooms, make this a highly recommended choice for anyone staying in the Hakone area.

The room was well-cleaned and had a pleasant scent. The bed was comfortable to sleep in. The meal service was smooth and offered with a smile, with unlimited soft drinks and alcohol. The food was delicious, and many desserts were homemade, allowing us to have a relaxed dinner for just the two of us and our child. All the women's amenities were provided, and I was able to comfortably use a Dyson hairdryer. It was a quick one-night stay. My child was very satisfied and said they wanted to go again! Thank you for the healing experience!

There also aren’t many options nearby to get food during the day, but you could take the cruise/cable car to some of the more popular locations in the area. Make sure to call several days ahead of time to book a spot on the free shuttle from Odawara station—it fills up and bus transport isn’t as easy.

Hotel is far away from any restaurants, so i would recommend to take both the dinner and breakfast package!
Great food both dinner and breakfast, loved it!
reviews.response:
Thank you for staying with Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori.
We are very glad to hear that you enjoy your stay. The buffet menu is changinf for each season. So we hope you enjoy different meal when you vist our hotel again.
We look forward to seeing you again. Thank you for your review.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.positives: Location

reviews.title: A nice break from the big cities
reviews.text:
This is like a luxury spa 
Onsens  to relax after a big day exploring 
We booked the private onsen so the kids could enjoy 
Breakfast and dinners were delicious 
Location awesome right next to the boat and skytrain and bus to get you to Odawara
reviews.response:
Thank you for staying with Hakone, Ashinoko Hanaori.
We are glad our facilities and location were able to make your trip enjoyable.
Our private onsen could be book in advanced, if you are visiting us again please let us know so we can make your stay more convenient.

Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori, Accommodation Section.

reviews.title: Very enjoyable stay
reviews.positives: Room was comfortable, but on the small side (not much space for two suitcases). Food (breakfast and dinner) were good quality with lots of variety, but the same every day.
reviews.negatives: I did not like having to book in for all meals via a QR-code. I would have liked a more personal option

reviews.title: Great experience
reviews.text: Hotel nearby the sightseeing point, very convenient and good to reduce the traffic time. Hotel staff are helpful and nice. Reserving shuttle bus is much time-saving for approaching hotel and leaving.
reviews.response: Thank you for staying at Hakone-Ashinoko Hanaori. We are happy to hear that you enjoyed your stay in Hakone. We look forward to seeing you again soon.
reviews.room_type: Casual Twin Room with Open-air Bath

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.positives: Great views. Friendly and helpful staff
reviews.negatives: Pillows weren’t comfortable.

reviews.title: Amazing hotel, great staff, perfect view and ideal location
reviews.text: Everything went well at the hotel for us. Do take the lake facing option since the view is perfect. Kind and smiling staff. Dinner and breakfast is same everyday and it is buffet style. There are some limited vegetarian options, but apart from that, I could not have expected a better hotel in the area.
reviews.response:
Thank you very much for staying with us at Ashinoko Hananori in Hakone, Japan.
We are very happy to hear that you enjoyed your stay with us.
All of our staff will continue to make every effort to provide you with the best stay in the future.
We look forward to welcoming you again.
Thank you very much for your nice comment.

reviews.title: Great location and service
reviews.positives: Great location near the pirate boat port and tram station. Rooms were nice and clean. Service was excellent. We had a private onset which was great. Rooms were a bit cramped but we didn’t spend a lot of time there so it wasn’t a big issue for us.
reviews.negatives: The breakfast and dinner buffets had a wide variety of food and great selection. Only issue was it was hard to find food served hot as most were room temperature.

reviews.title: Beautiful Romantic Hotel In Hakone
reviews.text:
Our stay was truly great in Hanaori! 
The staff is so sweet and the hotel is stunning. Great public onsen.
One thing could have been better is the breakfast buffet although there is a lot of variety, I'm just very picky at breakfast :) AMAZING HOTEL
reviews.response:
Thank you very much for staying with us at Ashinoko Hananori in Hakone, Japan.
We are very happy to hear that you enjoyed your stay with us.
Our meals change according to the four seasons in Japan. We hope you will enjoy your stay again when you return.
All of our staff will continue to make every effort to provide you with the best stay in the future.
Thank you very much for your kind words.
We look forward to welcoming you again.

reviews.title: Beautiful, romantic getaway
reviews.text: We loved it here.  Public onsen with outdoor baths under the sky were beautiful, views over the water were wonderful, dinner was delicious and great breakfast options too.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.negatives: The dinner and breakfast reservation had to be made by yourself. It would be more considerate if the time slot is asked by the staff during check in.

reviews.title: Gorgeous
reviews.text: Beautiful location right on the lake, with footbaths available outdoors overlooking. Restaurant is really pretty and food was excellent, also really good drinks menu.

reviews.title: Really enjoyed this hotel - exceeded my expectations!
reviews.positives: Beautiful facilities and view of the mountains and lake. Very well-designed and clean. Close to the lake and pirate cruise ship port. Great buffet with a lot of selection.
reviews.negatives: There’s not a lot of room for luggage in the rooms.

reviews.title: Good onsen resort
reviews.text: Walking distance from Togendai station.
reviews.response:
Thank you for your excellent review of your recent stay. I am delighted to read that you had great experience in Hanaori and our staff had taken such great care of you! Once again, thank you for your time and we hope to have pleasure to welcome you and your family again on your another vacation.
yours sincerely,

reviews.title: Relaxing and beautiful
reviews.positives: Location, onset and food
reviews.negatives: Rooms were small and only a few had a view. Need more shuttle services to various locations or activities initiated or conducted by the hotel like treks etc.

reviews.title: First time in Hakone
reviews.text: A tad bit out of the way but the facility is excellent, the staff are amazing and food is incredible. Can't recommend this place enough.

reviews.room_type: Casual Twin Room with Open-air Bath

reviews.title: One of the highlights of our trip to Japan
reviews.positives: Perfect location for a day trip to Hakone: the Pirate ship and the cable car to the volcano is just one minute walk from the hotel. The option of free transfer is a great advantage. The dinner and breakfast were very diverse, a lot to choose from. And the Onsen of course😍 We were a bit timid at first to go to the public Onsen, but it was totally worth it: it was not many people and the outdoor baths were absolutely stunning, even in the evening the view of the garden was very relaxing.
reviews.negatives: The automatic check-in machine extremely clumsy, dusty surfaces in the rooms (but as we noted it’s very common everywhere in Japan)

reviews.title: Hakone Ashinoko hanaoki
reviews.text: It was only lovely hotel with onsen baths and outdoor baths too. The place was very well maintained. There were a lot of details in the way the hotel was decorated. Food was good too. We also found out that they provide bus shuttles to train stations.
reviews.response:
Thank you for your review of your recent stay. I am delighted to read that you had a great experience and enjoyed everything's in Hanaori. Thank you for your time and we hope to have pleasure to welcome you again on your another vacation.
Yours Sincerely,

reviews.title: An extraordinary stay!
reviews.text:
This is by far the best hotel I’ve stayed at; you live the full experience: you have a great view, great room, you get to wear your ‘sumuye’ and your ‘yukata’ at all times in the hotel, you have the onsen and you get to book your private onsen as well (highly recommend the private)!  

I will definitely recommend and I’d like to come back for sure in my next trip to Japan!
reviews.response: Thank you for your exceptional review of Hakone Ashinoko Hanaori. We are delighted to hear that you had an excellent experience and enjoyed our beautiful rooms. Furthermore, it is grateful you enjoyed the private onsen and traditional Japanese clothing like Yukata and Samue. We appreciate your feedback and hope to welcome you back soon. Best regards,

reviews.title: Lovely Stay in Hakone
reviews.positives: Lovely stay in Hakone. We really enjoyed the private onsen, which was perfect for relaxing. The dinner and breakfast were both delicious and beautifully presented. There is also an outdoor foot onsen, which was such a nice touch. The hotel is in a great location, close to all the main Hakone attractions, making it very convenient for sightseeing. A wonderful experience overall.
reviews.negatives: Nothing

reviews.title: Amazing stay!
reviews.text:
Amazing stay! Too bad the weather didnt hold up. Regardless it was a lovely stay. Management was kind enough to change some parts of the breakfast as we stayed for more than 1 night. 

Other than that, the environment is awesome.
reviews.response:
Thank you for staying with Hakone Ashinoko HANAORI.
We are very pleased to hear that you enjoyed your stay with us. We hope that you will come again on a fine day and enjoy the scenery of Hakone.
We look forward to welcoming you again.
Thank you very much for your contribution.

reviews.title: Very good experience and relaxing stay
reviews.positives: Well appointed property. Lovely view and easy to access the cable car and boats. The food was very good and it was nice that breakfast and dinner were included as there isn’t anywhere else to get meals in the vicinity.
reviews.negatives: Only negative is that there is t anything else really around it. But if you get in the cable car and go back up or take the boat you can reach other areas that have shopping, restaurants and cafes.
